projects.title: Mastermind
content: Mastermind is a code-breaking game for two players. The game is played using:
- a decoding board, with a shield at one end covering a row of four large holes, and twelve (or ten, or eight, or six) additional rows containing four large holes next to a set of four small holes;
- code pegs of six different colors (or more; see Variations below), with round heads, which will be placed in the large holes on the board; and
- key pegs, some colored black, some white, which are flat-headed and smaller than the code pegs; they will be placed in the small holes on the board.

This was the first 'real' task we were given in college. We had to implement this game (with mutliplayer, 1 code master and one guesser) in Java. Every week the next piece of the puzzle was given, in total there were 5 or so. Me and my teammembers worked every Friday after receiving the next task together in on of my colleagues' place. I and one of the Arthur's did most of the finicky code. This is thus far the best team that I have ever had the honour to work in. We had our differences, we were argued, ... but we pulled through. I can only remember them as great friends.
Thanks to our proactive approach and great teamwork we finished all tasks 2 weeks before all the other teams.

projects.title: This Site V2
content: This site has gone through several version already. In my last attempt I tried to go for a minimalistic style. Code is not fully optimized (yet). Made with React.js and planning to add a GraphQL JavaScript backend.

projects.title: Post It
content: This project is part of the Web Application IV course for the Bachelor of Applied Informatics at the Ghent University College HoGent (Academic year 2019-2020). Post It is a sort of forum that can be used to ask questions, share ideas,... whatever you would like. The front-end was made using Angular 9 and the backend using C# (C-sharp).
This was the first time I worked with Three.js. I would love to do some more with Three.js or any other OpenGL library.

projects.title: PV / DA
content: These were websites I made for 2 solicitations. One is pure vanilla JavaScript (no frameworks,...) and the other in Angular. Worked less than the required time on them. Didn't get the job, probably due to mutliple factors, one of which Covid-19 (2 rather small companies). ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

projects.img_alt: Picture of tic tac toe game, x won 😃
projects.title: Tic Tac Toe
content: During my bachelorstudies I had a JavaScript (es6) examination coming up. Together with my little brother (I was teaching him JavaScript while learning it myself) we decided to create the classic tic tac toe (noughts and crosses, X's and O's, ...) game.
Whilst the game is quite simple to make. Our dreams were gushing with the games we could create next. The only thing stopping us was ourselves.

I remember stumbling here and there, but we managed to pull through. I cleared the exam first try and have very fond memories of these times. I will always hold these memories close to my heart, love you lil bro ❤️.
